'''T'surr''' was a [[Temperature/Legends|cold]] and windswept high-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Nuon e Safyd sector/Legends|Nuon e Safyd sector]]. Most of the planet's lifeforms had excellent low-light vision, and the harsh conditions of the world gave rise to a [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] also known as the [[T'surr (species)|T'surr]]. T'surr [[Gender/Legends|males]] often left their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] for the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], but their planet was never represented in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] or the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==Description==
T'surr was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="UAA" /> located in the [[T'surr system/Legends|T'surr system]], a part of the [[Nuon e Safyd sector/Legends|Nuon e Safyd sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Located far from its weak [[Star/Legends|sun]] that provided little light, T'surr was a [[Temperature/Legends|cold]] and windswept high-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] [[Desert/Legends|desert]] world.<ref name="UAA" /> Most of the [[Species/Legends|creatures]] that evolved on T'surr had excellent vision in low-light conditions.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|266|T'surr}}</ref> Species not native to T'surr found the planet's high gravity daunting and oppressive.<ref name="UAA" />

==History==
Although T'surr's native [[T'surr (species)|species]] of the same name could already be found off-world by [[1032 BBY/Legends|1032 BBY]],<ref name="Aflame1">[[Knight Errant 1|''Knight Errant'' 1]]</ref> T'surr had no representation in either the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] or in [[New Republic Senate/Legends|that]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] due to the perceived nature of the T'surr as aggressive [[Death/Legends|killers]].<ref name="UAA" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|T'surr (species)}}
T'surr was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] T'surr species. The harsh living conditions of their homeworld made the T'surr into hardy survivors who cared little for other people and adopted [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] as a cultural norm.<ref name="UAA" /> Although the [[Gender/Legends|males]] were the dominant T'surr gender on their homeworld,<ref name="CSWE" /> the females had the ultimate authority in T'surr homes, making many of the males anxious to leave their home planet for the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. The T'surr spoke the [[T'surrese]] [[Language/Legends|language]].<ref name="UAA" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planet T'surr was created for ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', a [[Wizards of the Coast]] sourcebook for use with the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''. Prior to the book's release in [[April]] [[2003]], the planet was mentioned in a [[March 27]], 2003 [[Wizards.com]] preview of a section of the book detailing the T'surr species.<ref name="Wizards">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030327tsurr|text=T'surr: ''Ultimate Alien Anthology'' Preview #4}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the T'surr [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square P-12.<ref name="TEA" />
